seoTitle: Start a Microsoft Teams Meeting Instantly or Scheduled
metaDescription: Launch an instant Meet Now session or schedule a future Microsoft Teams meeting, then set up your audio and video before going live.
intro: Whether you need to jump on a call right now or plan a meeting for later, Teams gives you two clear paths to get a session running. This guide covers launching an ad-hoc meeting instantly and scheduling one through the calendar, plus configuring your camera and mic before you join.

steps.heading: Open the Calendar panel
steps.body: Click the Calendar section in the left-hand navigation to access both instant and scheduled meeting options.

steps.heading: Start an instant meeting
steps.body: Click Meet Now to launch a session immediately, then choose whether to start right away or copy the link to share first.

steps.heading: Schedule a future meeting
steps.body: Click New Meeting to open the event form, then fill in a title, attendee emails, and a specific time range.

steps.heading: Notify a channel
steps.body: Select specific team channels if you want the meeting link automatically broadcast to all channel members.

steps.heading: Check your camera and mic before joining
steps.body: On the pre-join screen, turn on your webcam, verify your microphone, and adjust background settings before clicking Join Now.

faq.question: What's the difference between Meet Now and New Meeting?
faq.answer: Meet Now launches an instant session immediately, while New Meeting opens the full scheduling form for a future date and time.

faq.question: Can I invite people who aren't in my organization?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add external attendee email addresses directly in the meeting scheduling form.

faq.question: Do I have to turn my camera on to start a meeting?
faq.answer: No, the pre-join screen lets you toggle your camera off before joining if you'd rather start audio-only.

faq.question: Can I check my audio before the meeting starts?
faq.answer: Yes, the pre-join landing screen includes device checks for both your microphone and camera.
